title: After Apple, India’s smartphone manufacturing boom enters new phase with Vivo JV
summary: Vivo is expanding smartphone manufacturing in India through a joint venture, potentially establishing a model for other Chinese phone makers to follow Apple's success in the region. This represents a significant shift in global smartphone manufacturing as companies diversify away from traditional production hubs.
content: Vivo's joint venture could become a template for Chinese smartphone makers in India.

zh.title: 苹果之后，印度智能手机制造业迎来Vivo合资新阶段
zh.summary: Vivo正通过合资企业扩大在印度的智能手机制造，可能为其他中国手机制造商建立效仿苹果在该地区成功的模式。这代表全球智能手机制造的重大转变，企业正在远离传统生产中心。

title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
summary: OpenAI's new GPT-5.6 model family will continue powering Microsoft's Copilot 365 suite of workplace apps, signaling continued partnership despite speculation about the companies' relationship.
content: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

zh.title: OpenAI称GPT 5.6是Microsoft Copilot 365的"首选模型" 尽管存在分手传言
zh.summary: OpenAI新推出的GPT-5.6模型系列将继续为Microsoft Copilot 365办公应用套件提供支持，尽管两家公司的关系引发猜测，但这表明双方继续保持合作关系。

title: Don’t want to invest in Elon Musk? Two new ETFs explicitly exclude him
summary: Two new ETFs have launched that explicitly exclude companies founded, controlled, or led by Elon Musk, including Tesla and SpaceX. This reflects growing investor appetite for portfolio customization based on founder preferences.
content: The new exchanged-traded funds exclude companies that are founded, controlled, or led by Elon Musk. That means no SpaceX or Tesla.

zh.title: 不想投资埃隆·马斯克？两只新ETF明确排除他的公司
zh.summary: 两只新ETF已推出，明确排除由埃隆·马斯克创办、控制或领导的公司，包括特斯拉和SpaceX。这反映了投资者对根据创始人偏好定制投资组合的日益增长的需求。

title: Microsoft’s carbon emissions went up 25 percent last year
summary: Microsoft's carbon emissions jumped 25 percent in 2025 to 34 million metric tons, driven primarily by datacenter expansion and changes to renewable energy purchasing practices. This threatens its goal to become carbon negative by 2030.
content:
Microsoft may once again be struggling to keep up with its own climate goals, according to its 2026 sustainability report. As reported by GeekWire, the report states that Microsoft's carbon emissions increased 25 percent in 2025, totalling 34 million metric tons "without select interventions." Microsoft says this was "driven primarily by the expansion of our datacenter infrastructure," as well as the company's decision last February to stop purchasing "non-additional, unbundled renewable energy certificates." 
Several years ago, Microsoft set itself a goal to be carbon negative by 2030, meaning it will need to remove more carbon emissions t …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 微软去年碳排放量增加了25%
zh.summary: 微软2025年的碳排放量增长25%至3400万吨，主要由数据中心扩展和可再生能源采购实践的变化驱动。这威胁到了该公司在2030年前实现碳负排的目标。
